description_long: This is a classic corded telephone featuring a rectangular handset with rounded edges and a coiled cord attached at the bottom. The handset is predominantly white or light gray, with a small, slightly raised rectangular section that may serve as a button or indicator, visible on one side. The body is smooth and solid, with a visible groove along the side for gripping. The coiled cord is flexible and connects the handset to a base or wall unit, though the base is not visible in these views. The overall design is utilitarian, typical of telephones found in homes or private offices from the late 20th century. The object is compact, lightweight, and made primarily of plastic with some internal metal and electronic components.
description: A compact, rectangular corded telephone handset with a coiled cord and a smooth, light-colored plastic exterior. It features a small raised section on one side and a groove for gripping.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main face of the handset, the coiled cord hanging below, and a small raised section on the right side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the handset's thickness, the groove for gripping, and the protruding coiled cord attachment.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, smooth back of the handset and the coiled cord attachment at the bottom.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the groove for gripping and the overall slim profile of the handset.
